scope: count-bucketed PyTorch torch.bmm for routed-MoE W2 dgrad and SwiGLU backward

probe.contract: Compare global max-count padded torch.bmm against count-bucketed torch.bmm for dy [experts, rows, 7168] x w2 [experts, 7168, 3072], with valid-row SwiGLU backward checks.

tiny_smoke.note: Correctness compares valid routed rows only; retained skip-mask padded rows are intentionally not semantically consumed.

promote: row-count bucketing as evidence that some BMM FLOP waste is real
prepacked bucketed BMM as a lower-bound hint for a future custom scheduler
demote: dynamic PyTorch count-bucketed torch.bmm for full-layer integration
decision.reason: Bucketed BMM reduces padded rows and prepacked BMM alone can be 5.6% faster at S2048 and 12.6% faster at S4096. But the dynamic PyTorch bucket path loses badly after index_select, per-bucket launch overhead, and scatter: about 9.8 ms at S2048 and 10.9 ms at S4096 for BMM+SwiGLU, versus 3.2 ms and 3.8 ms for the current global padded BMM+SwiGLU baselines.
decision.next: Do not integrate PyTorch count bucketing into the retained layer. Use the bucket results to shape a CK/CK-Tile or TileLang compact scheduler that preserves the prepacked row-count advantage while avoiding Python loops, index_select copies, and per-bucket scatter.
